


The term "mental terrorism" isn't widely recognized in psychology or psychiatry, but it seems to refer to the psychological manipulation and coercion that can occur in various contexts, including scams. Scams can have a significant impact on mental health, leading to emotional distress, anxiety, depression, and even PTSD [A](https://www.aarp.org/money/scams-fraud/mental-health-impact/?copilot_analytics_%3D%3D&citationMarker=9F742443-6C92-4C44-BF58-8F5A7C53B6F1) [B](https://www.verywellmind.com/emotional-responses-scammed-8708252?copilot_analytics_%3D&citationMarker=9F742443-6C92-4C44-BF58-8F5A7C53B6F1) [C](https://www.lloydsbankinggroup.com/insights/what-is-the-emotional-impact-of-fraud.html?copilot_analytics_%3D%3D&citationMarker=9F742443-6C92-4C44-BF58-8F5A7C53B6F1). Victims often feel embarrassed, ashamed, and guilty, which can exacerbate their emotional suffering [A](https://www.aarp.org/money/scams-fraud/mental-health-impact/?copilot_analytics_%3D%3D&citationMarker=9F742443-6C92-4C44-BF58-8F5A7C53B6F1) [B](https://www.verywellmind.com/emotional-responses-scammed-8708252?copilot_analytics_%3D&citationMarker=9F742443-6C92-4C44-BF58-8F5A7C53B6F1).

Scammers use psychological tactics to manipulate their victims, creating a sense of urgency, fear, or trust to exploit them [B](https://www.verywellmind.com/emotional-responses-scammed-8708252?copilot_analytics_%3D&citationMarker=9F742443-6C92-4C44-BF58-8F5A7C53B6F1). This manipulation can be seen as a form of "mental terrorism" because it involves using psychological pressure to achieve a harmful goal.

If you or someone you know has been affected by a scam, it's important to seek support from mental health professionals and report the scam to the appropriate authorities. Remember, it's not your fault, and help is available.
